title: Faucet leak (intermittent)
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Place towels or a bucket under the leak to protect the cabinet and flooring.
2) If safe, turn OFF the shut-off valve(s) under the sink by turning them clockwise.
3) Note where the water appears: from the spout, handle, supply line, or P-trap.
4) Check whether the leak happens only when the faucet is on or even when it is off.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water cannot be stopped with the shut-off valves
- Water is contacting electrical outlets or power strips
- Water is spreading beyond the sink cabinet
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the faucet and handle
- Photo under the sink showing shut-off valves and leak source
- Short video if the leak comes and goes

title: Faucet leak (continuous)
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Put a bucket or towels under the leak immediately.
2) If safe, turn OFF the shut-off valves under the sink.
3) Confirm whether the dripping stops after the valves are shut off.
4) Do not continue using the faucet until maintenance reviews it.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The leak continues after shut-off valves are closed
- Water is spraying rather than dripping
- Cabinet or floor is becoming soaked
What to send us (photos/video):
- Video showing the faucet dripping
- Photo of shut-off valves after turning them off
- Photo of any wet flooring or cabinet damage

title: Under-sink leak (P-trap / supply line)
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Put a bucket under the leak and dry the area to identify the drip source.
2) If safe, turn OFF the shut-off valve for the leaking line.
3) Check if the drip comes from the curved P-trap or from the supply line connection.
4) Do not store items under the sink until the area is dry.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is spraying or leaking rapidly
- Water is spreading beyond the cabinet
- You see swelling, mold, or damage inside the cabinet
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the leak source under the sink
- Photo of shut-off valves
- Video of active dripping if present

title: Toilet keeps running
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Remove the toilet tank lid and check whether the flapper is sealing properly.
2) Gently press the flapper down to see if the running stops.
3) Make sure the chain is not tangled or too short.
4) If the water level is high, lower the float slightly if you know how to do so safely.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is overflowing from the tank or bowl
- Water is leaking onto the floor
- The shut-off valve behind the toilet does not work
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo inside the tank
- Video showing the running water sound
- Photo of any water on the floor

title: Toilet clogged or weak flush
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Stop flushing repeatedly to avoid overflow.
2) Use a toilet plunger and make several firm plunges.
3) Wait a minute and see whether the water level drops.
4) If another toilet in the unit works normally, let us know.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The toilet is overflowing
- Dirty water is coming onto the floor
- Multiple toilets or drains are backing up
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the water level in the bowl
- Photo of any overflow on the floor

title: Toilet leaking at base
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Stop using the toilet until maintenance reviews it.
2) Place towels around the base to contain water.
3) If safe, turn OFF the toilet shut-off valve behind the toilet.
4) Do not attempt to tighten bolts or remove the toilet.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is spreading across the floor
- The toilet feels loose or rocks when touched
- The shut-off valve does not stop the leak
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the base of the toilet
- Photo of the shut-off valve
- Video showing where the water appears

title: Sink draining slowly or clogged
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Remove visible debris from the drain opening.
2) Run hot water for 30 to 60 seconds if the sink is not fully blocked.
3) Try using a sink plunger.
4) If accessible, check whether the P-trap may be catching debris.
5) Do NOT use chemical drain cleaners.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is backing up rapidly
- Multiple drains in the unit are clogged
- Water leaks under the sink while draining
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the sink basin with standing water
- Photo under the sink

title: Tub or shower draining slowly
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Remove visible hair or debris from the drain cover if safe to do so.
2) Run hot water briefly to see whether drainage improves.
3) Use a tub-safe plunger if you have one.
4) Avoid chemical drain cleaners.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water does not drain at all
- Water backs up into another drain
- You notice leaking around the tub or ceiling below
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of standing water in the tub or shower
- Photo of drain cover area

title: Low water pressure
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether the issue is affecting one fixture or multiple fixtures.
2) Unscrew and rinse the faucet aerator or shower head if you know how to do so safely.
3) Let us know if neighbors or other units are affected, if known.
4) Note whether hot water, cold water, or both have low pressure.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Pressure suddenly dropped across the whole unit
- Brown or cloudy water appears
- Pressure changes are accompanied by leaking
What to send us (photos/video):
- Short video showing the weak water flow
- Note whether hot, cold, or both are affected

title: No hot water
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Confirm whether only one fixture is affected or the whole unit has no hot water.
2) Let the hot water run for one to two minutes to check for delay.
3) If your unit has a water heater you can safely view, check whether it shows an error light or display.
4) Do not attempt repairs on the water heater.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- There is any smell of gas
- You see water leaking from the water heater
- There is no hot water throughout the unit for an extended period
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the water heater display if visible
- Photo of any warning lights or labels

title: Water heater leaking
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Keep clear of the water heater if there is active leaking.
2) If safe and you know where it is, turn OFF the cold-water supply to the heater.
3) Protect nearby belongings from water if you can do so safely.
4) Do not touch electrical components around the heater.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is spreading quickly
- You smell gas
- The leak is near electrical wiring or outlets
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the leak area
- Photo of the shut-off valve if accessible
- Video showing how fast the water is leaking

title: Water shut-off basics
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Look under the sink or behind the toilet for small shut-off valves.
2) Turn the valve clockwise to close it.
3) Stop turning if the valve resists strongly; do not force it.
4) Once shut off, watch whether the leak slows or stops.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The valve does not move safely
- Turning the valve causes more leaking
- You are not sure which valve controls the fixture
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the valve before and after turning
- Video showing whether the leak changes

title: Breaker tripped (single circuit)
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Unplug devices on the affected circuit.
2) At the breaker panel, flip the breaker fully OFF and then back ON once.
3) Plug devices back in one at a time.
4) Note which device causes the breaker to trip again, if any.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The breaker trips repeatedly
- You smell burning or see discoloration
- You see sparks, smoke, or hear buzzing
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the breaker label area
- Photo of the affected outlet or device

title: Outlet not working
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether another device works in the same outlet.
2) Look for a GFCI outlet nearby and press RESET once.
3) Check whether other outlets in the same room have power.
4) If safe, check for a tripped breaker.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The outlet feels warm
- You smell burning
- You see sparks or scorch marks
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the outlet
- Photo of any GFCI outlet you reset

title: Lights flickering
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether the bulb is loose and tighten it only when the switch is off and the bulb is cool.
2) Test whether other lights in the room are also flickering.
3) Note whether flickering happens when another appliance turns on.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- There is a burning smell
- The switch plate is warm
- Flickering affects multiple rooms suddenly
What to send us (photos/video):
- Short video of the flickering
- Photo of the switch and fixture

title: Light switch not working
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether the bulb itself works in another fixture.
2) Confirm whether the same light can be controlled from another switch.
3) Do not remove switch plates or touch wiring.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The switch crackles
- The switch feels warm
- You see sparks or smell burning
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the switch
- Video showing what happens when toggled

title: Partial power outage in unit
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether only one room or one set of outlets is affected.
2) Check the breaker panel for a tripped breaker.
3) Ask whether building common areas or neighboring units are also affected, if easy to confirm.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You smell burning
- Power loss is combined with sparks or smoke
- Medical devices or critical equipment are affected
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of breaker panel labels
- List of rooms or fixtures affected

title: Burning smell or warm outlet
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Stop using the outlet or switch immediately.
2) Unplug devices if it is safe to do so.
3) If safe, turn OFF the corresponding breaker.
4) Keep the area clear until maintenance responds.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You see smoke or sparks
- The outlet plate is hot
- The smell continues after power is turned off
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the outlet or switch from a safe distance
- Photo of breaker label if known

title: Smoke alarm chirping
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether the chirp is due to a low battery.
2) Replace the battery if the alarm design allows tenant battery replacement.
3) Press the test button once after replacing the battery.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The alarm continues chirping after battery replacement
- The alarm is sounding continuously
- The unit appears damaged or detached
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the alarm
- Photo of battery compartment if accessible

title: Sparks from outlet or fixture
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Stop using the outlet or fixture immediately.
2) If safe, turn OFF the breaker controlling that area.
3) Keep clear of the outlet or fixture and do not touch exposed components.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Sparks continue
- You smell burning or see smoke
- The wall or cover plate becomes hot
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo from a safe distance if possible
- Photo of breaker label if known

title: Furnace not heating
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check that the thermostat is set to HEAT and above room temperature.
2) Replace thermostat batteries if applicable.
3) Make sure the furnace power switch is ON.
4) Check whether the filter is heavily clogged if it is safely accessible.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You smell gas
- The furnace makes loud banging or grinding noises
- There is no heat during cold weather
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of thermostat settings
- Photo of furnace area from a safe distance

title: Air conditioner not cooling
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Set the thermostat to COOL and lower than the current room temperature.
2) Make sure return vents are not blocked by furniture.
3) Check whether the air filter is heavily clogged if it is safely accessible.
4) Note whether the unit is blowing warm air or no air.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You see ice buildup on lines or unit
- The system makes loud unusual noises
- There is water leaking around the unit
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of thermostat settings
- Photo of indoor unit or vent

title: No heat in winter
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Confirm thermostat is set to HEAT.
2) Close windows and doors to keep heat in.
3) Use safe temporary warmth measures already approved for the unit.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Outdoor temperatures are low and the unit has no heat
- There are vulnerable occupants at risk
- You smell gas or see electrical issues
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of thermostat display
- Note indoor temperature if known

title: Thermostat not responding
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Replace batteries if the thermostat uses them.
2) Check whether the display is blank or showing an error.
3) Confirm the HVAC breaker is on if safe to check.
4) Do not remove the thermostat from the wall.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The thermostat smells hot or burnt
- It repeatedly resets or shows errors
- There is no heating or cooling response at all
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of thermostat display
- Video of button presses if helpful

title: Unusual HVAC noise
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Note whether the noise is banging, rattling, squealing, or buzzing.
2) Check whether the noise starts only when heating or cooling turns on.
3) If safe, turn the system off to see if the sound stops.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The noise is loud and sudden
- There is a burning smell
- The system shakes or leaks water
What to send us (photos/video):
- Short video with sound
- Photo of the unit if visible

title: Water leaking near furnace or air handler
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Keep belongings clear of the leaking area.
2) If safe, turn OFF the HVAC system at the thermostat.
3) Use towels or a container to limit water spread.
4) Do not open service panels.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is near electrical connections
- The leak is rapid
- There is also no heating or cooling
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the leak area
- Video showing where water is coming from

title: Radiator or baseboard not heating
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Make sure the thermostat is calling for heat if the unit has one.
2) Confirm furniture or curtains are not blocking the radiator or baseboard.
3) If there is an accessible valve, note whether it appears closed, but do not force it.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The radiator leaks water
- There is no heat in cold weather
- You hear banging or see corrosion and leakage
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the radiator or baseboard
- Note whether only one room is affected

title: Bathroom or ventilation fan not working
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether a nearby GFCI outlet has tripped and reset it once.
2) Check whether the fan switch works normally.
3) Note whether the fan is completely silent or humming.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You smell burning
- The fan cover is hot
- The fan makes grinding noises
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of fan cover and switch
- Short video with sound

title: Dishwasher not draining
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Cancel the cycle and avoid running it again until checked.
2) Remove and rinse the bottom filter if it is safely accessible.
3) Check whether the kitchen sink is also draining slowly.
4) Look for standing water inside the tub.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water leaks onto the floor
- There is a burning smell
- The dishwasher repeatedly hums without draining
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of standing water
- Photo of filter area

title: Dishwasher leaking
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Stop the cycle and do not run it again.
2) Place towels around the leak.
3) Note whether the water is leaking from the door, underneath, or supply connection area if visible.
4) If safe, turn off the water supply to the dishwasher if separately valved and accessible.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is spreading across the floor
- Water is near outlets
- The leak continues when the dishwasher is off
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the leak location
- Video during or just after a cycle if safe

title: Refrigerator leaking water
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Move items away from water on the floor.
2) Check whether the leak may be coming from an ice maker line, defrost tray, or clogged drain.
3) Keep the fridge door closed as much as possible.
4) Do not pull the fridge out forcefully if the floor may be damaged.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is spreading quickly
- The fridge is also not cooling
- The leak is near electrical connections
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the water on the floor
- Photo of the back or bottom area if visible

title: Refrigerator not cooling
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Confirm the fridge is plugged in and the outlet has power.
2) Check the temperature setting.
3) Make sure vents inside the fridge are not blocked by food.
4) Ensure the door closes and seals properly.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The fridge is making loud clicking or buzzing
- The back of the unit feels unusually hot
- Food safety is becoming a concern
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of temperature setting
- Photo of fridge interior

title: Washer will not spin
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether the load is very unbalanced and redistribute if safe.
2) Confirm the lid or door is fully closed.
3) Note whether water is still inside the drum.
4) Do not force the door open if it is locked.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water remains in the drum and leaks out
- The machine smells burnt
- Loud banging occurs repeatedly
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of control panel or error code
- Video showing what happens when cycle starts

title: Washer leaking
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Stop the cycle and avoid further use.
2) Use towels to contain water if safe.
3) Note whether the leak comes from the front door, underneath, or hose area.
4) If safe, turn OFF the water supply valves to the washer.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is spreading across the floor
- The leak is rapid
- The leak is near electrical outlets or cords
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of leak source
- Photo of washer supply hoses if visible

title: Dryer not heating
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Clean the lint trap completely.
2) Confirm the dryer is set to a heated cycle.
3) Check whether the drum still spins even though clothes remain cold.
4) Make sure the external vent is not obviously blocked if visible and safely accessible.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You smell burning
- The dryer shuts off unexpectedly
- The exterior or plug becomes hot
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of control settings
- Note whether the drum spins normally

title: Oven not heating or showing error
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Confirm the oven is set to the correct mode and temperature.
2) If electric, check whether the breaker tripped.
3) Note any error code on the display.
4) Do not attempt to disassemble panels or heating elements.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You smell gas
- You see sparks or smoke
- The error code indicates a safety lockout
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of control panel and any error code
- Photo of the oven interior if relevant

title: Locked out of unit
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether another approved entry method is available.
2) Verify you are at the correct unit and building entrance.
3) Contact the approved emergency or after-hours contact listed by management.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- A child, vulnerable adult, or pet is locked inside
- There is a medical or safety emergency
- The door or lock appears damaged
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the lock if it appears damaged
- Your current location and contact number

title: Deadbolt or lock sticking
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Try the key slowly without forcing it.
2) If safe, use approved lock lubricant if management allows it.
3) Check whether the door alignment changes when you lift or pull gently on the handle.
4) Avoid forcing the key or handle.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The key bends or jams
- The door cannot be secured
- The lock is loose or partially detached
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the lock and strike plate
- Video showing the sticking behavior

title: Key stuck in lock
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Stop turning the key if it feels stuck.
2) Gently wiggle the key while keeping the door pressure neutral.
3) Do not use excessive force or pliers.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The key bends
- Part of the key breaks off
- The door cannot be locked or unlocked safely
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the key in the lock
- Photo of any visible damage

title: Smart lock battery dead or not responding
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether the smart lock keypad or indicator lights respond.
2) Replace batteries if the lock design allows tenant battery replacement.
3) Use approved backup entry methods if available.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The unit cannot be secured
- The lock is stuck in a partially open state
- The lock repeatedly fails after fresh batteries
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the lock exterior
- Video showing unresponsive keypad or error lights

title: Ants in unit
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Wipe up crumbs, spills, and standing water.
2) Store food in sealed containers.
3) If management allows, place bait traps in affected areas rather than spraying.
4) Note where ants are entering or clustering.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Ant activity is heavy or spreading quickly
- Ants are appearing in multiple rooms despite cleaning
- There are signs of structural moisture attracting pests
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the ants and where they are entering
- Note which rooms are affected

title: Possible rodent activity
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Store food in sealed containers and remove accessible food sources.
2) Note signs such as droppings, gnaw marks, or scratching sounds.
3) Do not handle droppings without proper precautions.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- You see an active rodent
- There are signs near electrical wiring
- The issue is in multiple areas of the unit
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of droppings or gnaw marks
- Note where and when activity is noticed

title: Possible bedbug activity
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Do not move bedding or furniture to other rooms.
2) Wash bedding on a hot cycle if appropriate and approved.
3) Bag affected linens to limit spread.
4) Avoid self-treatment that could spread infestation.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Multiple sleeping areas are affected
- You see clusters of bites and visible insects
- The issue appears to be spreading rapidly
What to send us (photos/video):
- Close-up photo of signs on mattress seams or bed frame
- Photo of any visible insects or stains

title: Ceiling stain or slow leak
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Move belongings away from the affected area.
2) Place a container under any active drip.
3) Take note of whether the stain grows during rain or after shower use above.
4) Do not puncture the ceiling unless specifically instructed by management.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water is actively dripping
- The ceiling bulges or sags
- Water is near lights or electrical fixtures
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the stain or drip
- Video if dripping is active

title: Active roof leak
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Move electronics and belongings away from the leak path.
2) Place buckets or towels to contain water.
3) If water is near electrical fixtures, turn off power to the affected area if safe.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The leak is rapid
- Water reaches electrical fixtures
- Ceiling bulges, sags, or cracks
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of leak area and any ceiling damage
- Video showing leak intensity

title: Window leak during rain
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Place towels along the window sill.
2) Close and latch the window fully.
3) Note whether water enters from the frame, sill, or top edge.
4) Do not caulk or seal the window yourself unless instructed.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- Water pools on the floor
- The wall or sill is swelling
- The leak reaches electrical outlets
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of leak path
- Video during rain if possible

title: Crack in wall or ceiling
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Take clear photos of the crack from different angles.
2) Note whether the crack appears new, wider than before, or growing.
3) Avoid hanging anything heavy from the affected area.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The crack is wide, growing quickly, or accompanied by sagging
- Doors or windows nearby suddenly stop working properly
- Pieces of ceiling or wall are falling
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photos with a ruler or coin for scale
- Note location and whether the crack changed recently

title: Door not closing or frame shifted
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Check whether the problem happens only in humid weather or all the time.
2) Do not force the door closed if it binds heavily.
3) Note whether the lock aligns properly with the strike plate.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The door cannot be secured
- The frame appears visibly cracked or separated
- Multiple doors/windows in the area shift suddenly
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the door edge and frame
- Video showing where it binds

title: Mold suspicion
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) Increase ventilation if possible by using approved fans or opening windows when appropriate.
2) Do not scrub large areas aggressively without guidance.
3) Note whether the area is dry, damp, or actively wet.
4) Check whether there is a recurring leak or condensation nearby.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- The area is large or spreading
- Occupants are experiencing significant respiratory symptoms
- Mold appears around an active leak or HVAC system
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo of the affected area
- Note size and whether there is an odor

title: Water and power emergency quick guide
text:
Tenant-safe troubleshooting (do in order):
1) If water is actively leaking, use the nearest shut-off valve if you know where it is and it is safe to access.
2) If water is near outlets, extension cords, or appliances, avoid touching electrical items and turn off power to the affected area only if safe.
3) Move valuables away from water and keep pathways clear.
4) Contact emergency services first for any immediate danger to people.
Stop and escalate immediately if:
- There is sparking, smoke, or gas smell
- Flooding is spreading quickly
- The issue creates immediate risk to occupants
What to send us (photos/video):
- Photo or video of the emergency area from a safe distance
- Note what shutoffs you used, if any
